channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: Rep. Ralph Norman backs Trump's plan to avoid government shutdown, pushes for Congressional term limits
item.description: Rep. Ralph Norman, a member of the House Freedom Caucus, supports President Trump's plan for Congress to pass a continuing resolution. Norman is pushing a Constitutional amendment to term limit members of Congress.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump is set to speak to crypto founders and investors at the White House on Friday
item.description: President Trump signed an order late Thursday creating a cryptocurrency strategic reserve using bitcoin seized through court proceedings, directing officials to find “budget-neutral” ways to grow it.
item.dc:creator: Deepa Shivaram, MPR News

item.title: New fines and awareness help reduce litter on Minnesota lakes, but advocates want more action
item.description: Lake advocates say a recent state law is helping curb the problem of trash left on Minnesota’s frozen lakes. But they're hoping for more action, including passage of a bill requiring state agencies establish a statewide “Keep It Clean” program and provide funding for local cleanup efforts.
item.dc:creator: Kirsti Marohn, MPR News

item.title: Judge orders Trump administration to speed payment of USAID and State Dept. debts
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — A federal judge on Thursday ordered the Trump administration to speed up its payment on some of nearly $2 billion in debts to partners of the U.S.
item.dc:creator: ELLEN KNICKMEYER and MICHAEL KUNZELMAN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Georgia lawmakers pass bill allowing Trump and others to recover costs of election meddling case
item.description: Georgia’s Senate has passed a bill that would allow President Donald Trump and more than a dozen people to seek compensation for legal bills stemming from an attempt to overturn his 2020 election loss in the state.
item.dc:creator: Charlotte Kramon, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: US judge orders Trump admin to pay portion of $2B in foreign aid by Monday
item.description: A U.S. judge on Thursday ordered the Trump administration to pay at least a portion of the nearly $2 billion in owed foreign aid for previously completed projects by 6 p.m. Monday.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Fox News Politics Newsletter: Dems vs. Dems
item.description: Welcome to the Fox News Politics newsletter, with the latest updates on the Trump administration, Capitol Hill and more Fox News politics content.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Border state lawmaker reveals what 'drastic' changes can be solidified with major congressional action
item.description: Life for many residents in border communities improved following swift policy changes by the Trump administration, Rep. Juan Ciscomani, R-Ariz., says.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump stresses Cabinet secretaries lead agency cuts, but keeps Musk as threat
item.description: President Trump on Thursday stressed that his Cabinet secretaries take the lead on making cuts to their agencies and staff, insisting that officials are empowered to make decisions before Elon Musk can step in. “I want the Cabinet members, go first, keep all the people you want.
item.dc:creator: Alex Gangitano, NewsNation

item.title: Judge declines to immediately spare USAID contractors from firings
item.description: A federal judge on Thursday declined to immediately spare U.S. Agency for International Development (USAID) contractors from mass firings, letting move forward a core part of the Trump administration’s effort to dismantle the agency. U.S.
item.dc:creator: Ella Lee, NewsNation

item.title: Tensions flare in House over Al Green censure
item.description: Tensions flared in the House on Thursday as lawmakers voted to censure Rep. Al Green (D-Texas) for his disruptive protest during President Trump’s address to Congress, prompting a dramatic scene on the House floor — and demands from Republicans for further retribution.
item.dc:creator: Emily Brooks, NewsNation

item.title: Federal judge reinstates labor board member fired by President Donald Trump
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — A federal judge ruled Thursday that President Donald Trump acted illegally when he fired a member of an independent labor agency, and the judge ordered that she be allowed to remain on the job. U.S. District Judge Beryl Howell in Washington, D.C.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL KUNZELMAN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Hamas treatment of hostages 'intolerable,' Trump envoy says
item.description: Hamas’ treatment of the hostages it has been holding in captivity in the Gaza Strip is "intolerable," U.S. envoy to the Middle East Steve Witkoff said.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: FDA nominee sidesteps questions on abortion pill, agency layoffs and other issues
item.description: President Donald Trump’s pick to lead the Food and Drug Administration largely sidestepped questions Thursday from senators about how he would handle numerous pressing issues before the agency, including recent layoffs, a canceled vaccine meeting and the continued availability of the abortion pill.
item.dc:creator: MATTHEW PERRONE, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: U.S. economy likely generated a solid 160,000 jobs last month, but Trump's trade wars cloud outlook
item.description: The U.S. labor market likely kept on churning out jobs last month, economists say, but the outlook is cloudy and getting cloudier as the Trump administration wages trade wars, purges federal employees and seeks to deport millions of immigrants.
item.dc:creator: Paul Wiseman, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Women's rights are under attack 30 years after leaders adopted a blueprint for equality, UN says
item.description: UNITED NATIONS (AP) — Thirty years after world leaders adopted a historic blueprint to achieve gender equality, a new United Nations report says women’s and girls’ rights are under attack and gender discrimination remains deeply embedded in economies and societies.
item.dc:creator: EDITH M. LEDERER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump to put tariff exemptions on certain goods from Canada, Mexico
item.description: On Thursday, President Trump signed an amendment exempting certain Canadian and Mexican goods from his 25% tariffs imposed earlier this week.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump raises doubts about NATO's mutual defense pact
item.description: President Trump on Thursday suggested the United States may not defend NATO allies that have not contributed enough to defense spending, casting doubt on a core aspect of the alliance.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Dem senator behind social media fail responds to Elon Musk's offer
item.description: Sen. Cory Booker said he will not be taking up Elon Musk on an offer after the senator took credit for a Democratic social media mashup against President Donald Trump.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: CIA lays off some recently hired officers as Trump shakes up intelligence community
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The Central Intelligence Agency will fire an unreleased number of junior officers as President Donald Trump's efforts to downsize and reshape the federal government reverberate through America's intelligence community.
item.dc:creator: DAVID KLEPPER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump signs tariff exemptions for certain imports from Canada, Mexico
item.description: President Trump on Thursday signed off on tariff exemptions for imports from Canada and Mexico that are covered under a 2020 North American trade agreement, marking the latest reversal of duties imposed earlier this week. Under the orders, goods compliant with the U.S.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: House votes to censure Texas Democrat Al Green for protest during Trump address
item.description: Texas Rep. Al Green, a Democrat, was officially censured Thursday in a vote in the House following his protest on Tuesday during President Trump’s address to a joint session of Congress.
item.dc:creator: Barbara Sprunt, MPR News

item.title: Mother of Abbey Gate victim says Biden ‘robbed’ family of justice
item.description: "If we can take one terrorist off the list of harming Americans, especially our soldiers, then that is a good day," Paula Knauss Selph told NewsNation.
item.dc:creator: Anna Kutz, NewsNation

item.title: Klobuchar, Smith call for answers about potential Indian Health Service office lease termination
item.description: U.S. Senators Tina Smith and Amy Klobuchar are pressing for answers regarding the potential termination of the Bemidji Area Indian Health Service’s lease, underlining treaty obligations and concerns for disruptions in health care services.
item.dc:creator: Melissa Olson, MPR News

item.title: Hamas disregards Trump’s ‘last warning’ over hostage release
item.description: Hamas' response is an apparent dismissal of President Donald Trump's "last warning" issued on social media.
item.dc:creator: Tom Dempsey, NewsNation

item.title: NYC Council Speaker Adrienne Adams to run for mayor
item.description: NEW YORK (PIX11) – New York City Council Speaker Adrienne Adams is joining the race for the mayor’s office, she's expected to announce on Thursday.
item.dc:creator: Erin Pflaumer, PIX 11

item.title: Mexican medical manufacturers warn tariffs will increase US health care costs
item.description: "We are worried about patients in hospitals because they are the ones who will be affected the most," said Alfredo Dueñez, president of Baja California's Medical Device Cluster.
item.dc:creator: Salvador Rivera, NewsNation

item.title: NPS takes down web pages dedicated to transgender activists and LGBTQ history
item.description: This is part of an ongoing move by the federal government to remove and alter National Park Service webpages related to LGBTQ history.
item.dc:creator: Neda Ulaby, MPR News

item.title: Nevada Democrats urge Lombardo to hire fired federal workers
item.description: Democratic leaders in the Nevada Assembly are urging Republican Gov. Joe Lombardo to hire federal workers laid off as part of recent firings from President Donald Trump and Elon Musk.
item.dc:creator: David Charns, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: State officials forecast higher energy prices because of Trump tariffs
item.description: Minnesota is heavily reliant on Canada for imports of natural gas, crude oil and electricity, all of which may be subject to a 10 percent tariff.
item.dc:creator: Dan Kraker, MPR News

item.title: Nevada Democrats react to Trump's speech; state Republicans applaud his vision
item.description: Democrats who represent Nevada in Washington, D.C., condemned what they see as a failure by President Donald Trump to lower prices for Nevada families, while state Republicans applauded his leadership the morning after his address to a joint session of Congress Tuesday night.
item.dc:creator: Greg Haas, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Trump administration pauses flow of intelligence to Ukraine that helps on battlefield
item.description: CIA Director John Ratcliffe called the suspension a “pause” and said it came after the disastrous meeting between Trump and Zelenskyy in the Oval Office last week. Ratcliffe said Trump wanted to know that Zelenskyy was serious about peace.
item.dc:creator: DAVID KLEPPER and DARLENE SUPERVILLE, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: San Diego Congress member pushes Trump for Nobel Peace Prize
item.description: A San Diego congressional representative is pressing the Norwegian committee who awards the prestigious Nobel Prizes to consider President Donald Trump to be the 2025 recipient of the peace prize.
item.dc:creator: Danielle Dawson, Fox 5

item.title: Nevada 'a perfect storm for homicides' in domestic violence cases, lawmakers told
item.description: FBI statistics show Nevada is "a perfect storm for homicides" and the state ranks third for men murdering women, the leader of SafeNest said Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: Greg Haas, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Bondi says she was misled on Epstein documents
item.description: Attorney General Pam Bondi says she was misled into believing she had all the files related to the Epstein investigations, and is now seeking additional information from the FBI.
item.dc:creator: Elizabeth Crisp, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Trump administration ramps up attacks as Zelenskyy thanks US
item.description: There is a growing chorus of Republicans calling for new Ukrainian leadership following Friday's Oval Office meeting.
item.dc:creator: Tom Dempsey, Fox 5

item.title: Judge extends block on Trump gender-affirming care federal funding ban
item.description: U.S. District Court Judge Lauren King ruled that President Trump’s executive orders violate the Constitution’s Fifth Amendment, which grants equal protection under the law.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, WFLA, NBC
